what_conformance_missed: LD12 proof rollup (debug_report._snyk_rollup) trusted the prose-supplied newFindings instead of recomputing from before/after counts; a real Snyk regression (before=0,after=5,newFindings=0) rendered as clean. PART A PASS on internally-consistent seeded fixtures.

LD12 deviation table (debug_report.build_deviation_table) set applied:true on drift PASS alone, not gated on the finding's route; under a finding_id collision a research finding rendered as a proven applied fix. PART A PASS on the happy-path fixtures.

Tier-2 apply park overwrote the Tier-1 escalate artifact (.kata/escalations/<task-id>.json) for the same task on the stateful-destroy-apply path; one of two human-required reasons could be silently lost. Per-build D98 (D119) passed it; the D117-D121 cross-cut ad-val caught it.
guard_ref: kata-orchestrate Tier-1-escalate-parks-and-returns + escalation.py write_escalation non-clobber guard

what_conformance_missed: debug_report _snyk_rollup did not floor effective_new at 0; a negative newFindings artifact could deflate the advisory regression total (could NOT flip clean=True). Per-build D98 (D117) passed it; the cross-cut ad-val caught it.

exec-safety registry completeness was human-enforced only (no test asserted every subprocess sink is registered); a shell=False sink could slip through unregistered. The mis-described kata_install row (D121) was a live instance. Cross-cut ad-val caught it.
